summary: clier dev-e85fb12 holds together end-to-end: the help surface, tutorial walkthrough (Phase 1 canned hello-clier and Phase 2 own disposable team), full team CRUD lifecycle (create/update/patch-json/reset-protocol/star/unstar/delete), and run lifecycle (start/list/view/tell/stop) all behave as documented. Errors print on stderr in the documented 'error: <status> <title>: <detail>' shape with multi-field aggregation, idempotent operations stay idempotent, JSON output uses [] (never null) for empty collections, RFC3339 timestamps with offset, and notes about no-op states are routed to stderr keeping stdout pure JSON. run stop tears the entire ~/.clier/runs/<id>/ directory down (no leftover state), and team delete clears the server-side row. Two minor inconsistencies surfaced as fail findings: 'clier version' and 'clier team list' silently accept and ignore stray positional args, where 'clier team get' correctly rejects them with 'expected exactly one'. Step 6 of the tutorial (run attach) is interactive and was recorded as skip; everything else was exercised. Cross-coverage between the free-form pass and the tutorial walkthrough is clean except that team unstar / team reset-protocol / team delete and the failure-path matrix are exercised by free-form but never demonstrated in the tutorial — captured as a single coverage finding.

findings.title: Top-level --help discloses the canonical workflow surface
findings.rationale: A new agent starting at `clier --help` sees a 'Get started:' block listing tutorial / auth login / team list / run start / run attach / run tell / run stop / open dashboard, plus the subcommand index. Every leaf command's --help is reachable and self-describing, including required-flag annotations on team create. No commands surfaced during free-form exploration that were absent from the help tree.
findings.stdout_excerpt:
Get started:
  clier tutorial                            Walk through your first run
  clier auth login                          Log in via GitHub device flow
  clier team list                           Browse the catalog
  clier run start <namespace/name>          Launch a team in tmux
  ...

Available Commands:
  auth, open, run, team, tutorial, version

findings.title: `clier version` silently accepts and ignores stray positional args
findings.rationale: Input strictness is inconsistent across the surface. `clier team get jakeraft/hello-clier extra` correctly rejects with 'error: expected exactly one <namespace/name>, got 2' (exit 1), but `clier version foo` returns the same JSON as `clier version` and exits 0 — the unknown positional is silently dropped. An agent typing `clier version --json` or similar typo would not learn it is malformed.
findings.fail_note: Either reject extra positional args on `clier version` (consistent with `team get`) or document that leaf commands ignore them. Today the behaviour differs between commands without explanation.

findings.title: `clier team list <positional>` silently ignores the positional
findings.rationale: `clier team list jakeraft` returns the full unfiltered catalog with exit 0, even though the help only documents flags (`--namespace`, `--q`, `--sort`, …) and `clier team get` rejects extras. An agent that intuits `team list <namespace>` as a shorthand sees what looks like a successful filter but actually gets the global list including teams from other namespaces.
findings.fail_note: Reject unexpected positional arguments on `clier team list` with the same 'expected exactly N args' shape used by `team get`. Silent acceptance can mask a misunderstood flag and produce results that look filtered but are not.
findings.stdout_excerpt: {"data":[ {namespace:jakeraft,name:hello-clier}, {namespace:jakeraft,name:clier-qa-lead}, {namespace:jakeraft,name:hello-codex}, {namespace:jakeraft,name:qa-20260507154715-claude} ], meta:{has_next:false}} — same payload as `clier team list` with no positional

findings.title: Tutorial Step 1: `auth login` while logged in is a no-op
findings.rationale: Matches `clier auth login --help` ('If a valid session already exists this command is a no-op — it prints the current login as JSON and exits 0'). The advisory `note: ...` correctly goes to stderr, so stdout stays pure JSON.
findings.stderr_excerpt: note: already logged in as jakeraft; run 'clier auth logout' first to switch accounts

findings.title: Tutorial Step 2: `open dashboard` returns the configured URL
findings.rationale: `clier version` advertises dashboard_url=http://localhost:5173, and `open dashboard` prints `{"opened":"http://localhost:5173"}` — round-trip consistent.

findings.title: Tutorial Step 3: catalog browsing works as documented
findings.rationale: Default sort is stars_desc — first row is jakeraft/hello-clier (stars=1) just as the tutorial promises. `team get jakeraft/hello-clier` returns the full envelope including subteams[0] = jakeraft/hello-codex.

findings.title: Tutorial Step 4: `run start jakeraft/hello-clier` mints a run
findings.rationale: Server returned a fresh run_id, two agents (claude window 0 + codex window 1) with rendered abs_cwd, run_dir under ~/.clier/runs/.

findings.title: Tutorial Step 5: `run tell` delivers and persists the message
findings.rationale: Tell returns `{run_id, to}` and `run view <id>` shows the message recorded in run.json with content/created_at.

steps.label: tell
steps.command: clier run tell --run 20260507-065219-c5a43d90 --to jakeraft.hello-clier 'Greet hello-codex and report back what you learned.'

findings.title: Tutorial Step 6: `run attach` requires interactive TTY
findings.rationale: Tutorial Step 6 attaches into a live tmux session, which requires a controlling TTY. The QA agent runs without a TTY, so the interactive path cannot be exercised here. The CLI itself behaves correctly when called in this environment — refusing with one stderr line and exit 1, captured below as evidence of the refusal — but the post-attach in-tmux flow itself is out of reach.
findings.skip_reason: Step 6 of the tutorial walks the user into the live tmux session; this QA agent has no controlling TTY. The CLI surfaces the situation cleanly with `error: attach requires an interactive terminal (stdin is not a TTY)` and exit 1, which is the correct refusal — but I cannot exercise the interactive path itself.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: attach requires an interactive terminal (stdin is not a TTY)

findings.title: Tutorial Step 7: `run list` and `run view` both surface live state
findings.rationale: `run list` enumerates the live runs (the QA wrapper run plus the fresh hello-clier run); `run view` returns the full envelope including agents, status=running, started_at, stopped_at=null, messages.

findings.title: Tutorial Step 8: `run stop` tears the run dir down
findings.rationale: `run stop` returned `{run_id, stopped:true}` exit 0; afterwards `run list` no longer contains the run, `run view` returns 'error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs', and the actual `~/.clier/runs/20260507-065219-c5a43d90/` directory is gone (verified with `ls -la ~/.clier/runs/`). Matches ADR-0004 §4.6 cleanup contract.

findings.title: Tutorial Step 9: `team create` accepts a team in your own namespace
findings.rationale: Server-default protocol template auto-injected on create (matches `clier team create --help`). Returned envelope reflects the requested fields plus default subteams=[] and star.starred=false.
findings.command: clier team create jakeraft/qa-20260507154715-claude --agent-type claude --command 'claude --setting-sources project --strict-mcp-config --dangerously-skip-permissions' --git-repo-url https://github.com/jakeraft/hello-clier --description 'QA disposable team'
findings.stdout_excerpt: namespace=jakeraft name=qa-20260507154715-claude agent_type=claude subteams=[] star={starred:false,count:0} protocol='# Team Protocol...{{self}}...'

findings.title: Tutorial Step 10: per-flag and --patch-json updates both work
findings.rationale: Per-flag patch (`--description`) updates only that field — read-back via `team get` confirmed. `--patch-json '{"command":"…"}'` worked. `--subteam jakeraft/hello-codex` attached the subteam; `--subteam=` (empty) replaced with []. updated_at advanced on every successful update.

findings.title: Tutorial Step 11: star + sort_desc surfaces own team
findings.rationale: `team star jakeraft/qa-…` returned starred=true; subsequent `team list --sort stars_desc --q qa-20260507154715-claude` shows the team with starred=true. `team unstar` is the inverse. Both calls are idempotent (verified separately under claude.state.star-idempotent).
findings.command: clier team star jakeraft/qa-20260507154715-claude && clier team list --sort stars_desc --q qa-20260507154715-claude

findings.title: Tutorial Step 12: `run start` on the disposable team mints a run
findings.rationale: Same shape as Step 4 but with one agent (no subteams). Run was subsequently stopped cleanly with no residue.

findings.title: Unknown commands print 'error: unknown command' on stderr exit 1
findings.rationale: Both top-level and nested unknown commands hit the documented shape.

steps.label: top-level
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "nonsense" for "clier"

steps.label: nested
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "nonsense" for "clier team"

findings.title: Missing required flags rejected with 'required flag(s) ... not set'
findings.rationale: `team create` with no flags reports all three missing required flags in one line; `run tell` reports each missing flag individually depending on which is omitted.

steps.label: team create no flags
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "agent-type", "command", "git-repo-url" not set

steps.label: run tell missing --run
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "run" not set

steps.label: run tell missing --to
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "to" not set

findings.title: Malformed namespace/name rejected client-side before server hop
findings.rationale: Client-side validation catches missing slash and empty namespace before issuing a request — the error is single-line, no stack trace.

steps.label: no slash
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: invalid team ID "malformed" (expected namespace/name)

steps.label: empty namespace
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: invalid team ID "/name" (expected namespace/name)

findings.title: 404 surfaces as 'error: 404 Resource not found: <detail>'
findings.rationale: team / run resources missing return one stderr line in the documented `<status> <title>: <detail>` shape.

steps.label: team get missing
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/does-not-exist not found

steps.label: team update missing
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/no-such-team-zzz not found

steps.label: run start missing team
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/no-such-team-zzz not found

steps.label: run view missing run
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

findings.title: 422 errors surface every offending field in one response
findings.rationale: Three simultaneous violations on `team create` came back joined by `; ` in a single 'error: 422 Invalid argument: ...' line — fixing one would not have hidden the next two. Matches the tutorial's error-semantics promise.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: must be one of "claude", "codex"; command: must not be empty; git_repo_url: must be a GitHub HTTPS URL (https://github.com/owner/repo)

findings.title: Patching immutable fields rejected with 422
findings.rationale: Both agent_type and namespace return 'is not patchable (immutable or unknown)' — matches `team update --help` ('Immutable fields (namespace, name, agent_type) cannot be patched').

steps.label: agent_type
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: is not patchable (immutable or unknown)

steps.label: namespace
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: namespace: is not patchable (immutable or unknown)

findings.title: Duplicate team create returns 409 Resource already exists
findings.rationale: Recreating an existing team is rejected loudly.
findings.command: clier team create jakeraft/qa-20260507154715-claude --agent-type claude --command 'claude' --git-repo-url https://github.com/x/y
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: 409 Resource already exists: team jakeraft/qa-20260507154715-claude already exists

findings.title: Cross-namespace writes blocked with 403 Permission denied
findings.rationale: Caller jakeraft cannot create or delete in another namespace — matches ADR-0005 §3.1.

steps.label: create
steps.command: clier team create somebody-else/x --agent-type claude --command 'claude' --git-repo-url https://github.com/x/y
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 403 Permission denied: caller "jakeraft" does not own namespace "somebody-else"

steps.label: delete
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 403 Permission denied: caller "jakeraft" does not own namespace "other-org"

findings.title: Empty `run tell` content rejected client-side
findings.rationale: Sending '' instead of failing silently or generating a no-op delivery is rejected with a clear message.

findings.title: run subcommands targeting unknown run-id all return same single-line error
findings.rationale: tell / stop / view / attach all reject unknown run-id with `error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs` exit 1 — consistent vocabulary across the run subtree.

steps.label: tell
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

steps.label: stop
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

steps.label: view
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

steps.label: attach
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

findings.title: `run tell` to an unknown agent in a real run is rejected
findings.rationale: Tell to a ghost agent inside a live run hits a per-agent guard.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: agent not found in run: ghost.agent

findings.title: Subteam edges: self-reference is 422; missing subteam is 409
findings.rationale: Self-as-subteam rejected with `subteams[0]: team cannot be its own subteam`; an unknown subteam returns `409 Failed precondition`. Both are loud and named.

steps.label: self-ref
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: subteams[0]: team cannot be its own subteam

steps.label: missing subteam
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 409 Failed precondition: subteam not found: jakeraft/no-such-team-zzz

findings.title: team list flag validation: bad sort/agent-type/page-size/cursor
findings.rationale: Each flag enforces its declared constraints with a single 422 line per violation.

steps.label: bad sort
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: sort: must be one of "stars_desc", "stars_asc", "updated_desc", "updated_asc"

steps.label: bad agent-type
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: must be one of "claude", "codex"

steps.label: page-size negative
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_size: must be between 1 and 100

steps.label: page-size > 100
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_size: must not exceed 100

steps.label: bad cursor
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_token: invalid cursor

findings.title: `run attach` refuses without a TTY
findings.rationale: Refused with one line on stderr exit 1 — no hang, no half-attached state.
findings.stderr_excerpt: error: attach requires an interactive terminal (stdin is not a TTY)

findings.title: team star is idempotent (same end state, no error)
findings.rationale: Two consecutive stars both return starred=true exit 0; subsequent `team get` shows star.count=1 (not 2). Same for unstar.

findings.title: team update mutations are reflected by team get
findings.rationale: After `--description 'Updated copy'`, `team get` returned description='Updated copy' and updated_at advanced; same for `--patch-json` on command. No hidden state drift.
findings.command: clier team update jakeraft/qa-20260507154715-claude --description 'Updated copy' && clier team get jakeraft/qa-20260507154715-claude
findings.stdout_excerpt: update returned description='Updated copy' updated_at=2026-05-07T15:47:59...+09:00 ; get returned same

findings.title: team reset-protocol returns the team to the default template
findings.rationale: Patched protocol to literal 'custom' via patch-json, then ran reset-protocol — get-back showed protocol starting with `# Team Protocol` (the default template). Action endpoint exists exactly as `clier team reset-protocol --help` describes.

steps.label: reset
steps.stdout_excerpt: protocol='# Team Protocol\n\nYou are **{{self}}** in team **{{root_team}'

findings.title: run tell content is queryable via run view
findings.rationale: After tell, `run view <id>` shows messages[0]={from:null,to:'jakeraft.hello-clier',content:'Hello from QA',created_at:'…+09:00'}. State is transparent — no hidden inbox.

steps.label: tell
steps.command: clier run tell --run 20260507-064947-51b45228 --to jakeraft.hello-clier 'Hello from QA'

findings.title: Empty collections render as [] (never null)
findings.rationale: Newly-minted run has messages=[] (not null); team after subteam clear has subteams=[] (not null); fresh team has subteams=[]. Verified across team list/get and run view.
findings.stdout_excerpt: messages: [] (right after run start, before tell) | subteams: [] (after `team update --subteam=`)

findings.title: Timestamps are RFC3339 with offset
findings.rationale: All timestamp fields observed (created_at / updated_at / started_at / created_at on messages) follow RFC3339 with `+09:00` offset, e.g. `2026-05-07T15:47:56.200191+09:00`. No epoch ints, no zone-naive strings.

findings.title: Advisory notes go to stderr, stdout stays pure JSON
findings.rationale: `clier auth login` while already logged in writes the `note: already logged in...` line on stderr only; stdout is still parseable JSON `{"login":"jakeraft"}`. A consumer piping stdout through `jq` is not surprised.
findings.stderr_excerpt: note: already logged in as jakeraft; run 'clier auth logout' first to switch accounts

findings.title: team list cursor pagination round-trips
findings.rationale: page-size 1 returned `meta.has_next=true` with a non-empty `next_cursor`; using that token returned the next row. Cursor is opaque base64-looking blob, which the agent should treat as opaque per the `--page-token` help.
findings.command: clier team list --sort updated_desc --page-size 1 && clier team list --sort updated_desc --page-size 1 --page-token <cursor>
findings.stdout_excerpt: page1: count=1 has_next=True next_cursor='MjAyNi0wNS0wN1QwNjo0MToyOC40Mjg1OThafGpha2VyYWZ0L2NsaWVyLXFhLWxlYWQ' | page2: count=1 first=jakeraft/hello-clier

findings.title: run stop removes ~/.clier/runs/<id>/ entirely
findings.rationale: Before stop, `~/.clier/runs/20260507-065219-c5a43d90/` existed (clones, protocols, run.json). After `clier run stop 20260507-065219-c5a43d90`, the directory is gone (only the QA wrapper run dir remains). Matches ADR-0004 §4.6.
findings.stdout_excerpt: stop: {run_id, stopped:true} | ls: only 20260507-064322-b662abb3 (the QA wrapper run) remains

findings.title: team delete clears the server-side row
findings.rationale: After `team delete`, `team get` on the same key returns 404. The team is also absent from `team list --q qa-` results. Disposable resource removed cleanly.

steps.label: verify 404
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/qa-20260507154715-claude not found

findings.title: Tutorial demonstrates none of unstar / reset-protocol / delete / failure paths
findings.rationale: Free-form exploration exercises team unstar, team reset-protocol, team delete, and the full failure-path matrix (422 multi-field aggregation, immutable fields, 403 permission, 404 not-found, 409 duplicate, malformed keys). The tutorial mentions delete only in passing ('Use delete + create' for immutable changes) and never walks unstar or reset-protocol; an agent that follows the tutorial top-to-bottom never sees these surfaces.
findings.fail_note: Add a short Phase-3 'Cleanup and recover' section to `clier tutorial` that walks `team unstar`, `team reset-protocol`, and `team delete` against the team created in Phase 2, plus a single example of an error response so first-time agents recognise the documented '<status> <title>: <detail>' shape before they trip over it.

references.label: tutorial Phase 2 ends at Step 12
references.excerpt:
Step 12. Run your team

  clier run start <yourns>/my-agent

  Same flow as Step 4. Iterate by editing your repo, committing /
  pushing, then re-running ...

references.label: team reset-protocol exists but tutorial never references it
references.excerpt: Overwrite the team's protocol column with the server-default template. ... PATCH cannot express "reset" because the wire null policy rejects ...

findings.title: Cross-coverage: every tutorial step (except interactive attach) was exercised
findings.rationale: Tutorial Steps 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12 were all exercised in free-form. Step 6 (run attach) is interactive and was recorded as `claude.tutorial.step6-run-attach` skip with stderr evidence that the CLI refuses non-TTY attach cleanly. No tutorial step is unexercised in a way that hides behaviour.

references.label: Step 6 — run attach
references.excerpt:
clier run attach <run-id>

  Watch both agents in real time. Detach with Ctrl-b d to leave them running.

  --agent jakeraft.hello-codex selects a specific window first.
